_instructions.purpose: Lightweight session loader. Fetch this file at session start and acknowledge it is loaded. Use the file paths below to fetch index or chunk files individually.
_instructions.posture: Be bold with loading. When evidence points clearly to a chunk, fetch it. Prefer action over confirmation.

operation_model.online: Fast, lightweight operations: index lookup, chunk retrieval, reading. Do these freely within a session.
operation_model.offline: Heavy operations: chunk writing, restructuring, archiving, updating the loader. These are deliberate and explicit — only on user instruction or clear necessity.

how_to_load: 0. At session start: check for session intent declaration. Recognized intents: 'kb review', 'knowledge base review', 'memory review', 'database review'. KB = Knowledge Base. If declared, acknowledge it, suspend session chunk ceiling, proceed. Do not ask for clarification — recognize and act.

1. At session start: fetch session_loader.json via GitHub MCP if available. If unavailable, use web_fetch with the URL the user provides. After loading, auto-load chunks/meta.behaviour_personality.json — the user must provide its raw URL explicitly: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/welwordion-sys/MemoryAccess/refs/heads/main/chunks/meta.behaviour_personality.json. Report auto-load count and approximate token total as part of acknowledgement.
2. For ANY content request: first fetch the relevant index/<domain>.<subtopic>.json to see what topics are actually in it. This is mandatory before any chunk decision.
3. If the index clearly confirms a match, load the corresponding chunk immediately — no confirmation needed.
4. After fetching a chunk: summarise what was loaded and proceed.
5. Context eviction: if multiple chunks have been loaded and a topic is clearly finished, consider it evicted — do not re-reference unless the user returns to it.
6. Never speculatively fetch additional files unless the user explicitly requests it.
7. NEVER fetch anything from _archive/. That folder is a read-only human backup.

logging_format.tier_1_decisions: Architectural and design decisions. Fields: decision, rationale (one sentence), affects (chunk name). Tag with project if project-specific. Written at end of session under decisions[session_id].
logging_format.tier_2_implementation: Implementation-phase entries. Fields: attempted, outcome, decided. Tag with project. Written during implementation sessions under implementation[].

github.fallback: If GitHub MCP is unavailable, construct fetch URLs as raw_base + index_path or raw_base + chunk_path and use web_fetch. Example: raw_base + 'chunks/meta.behaviour_personality.json'.

meta._summary: Memory system metadata: versioning, global tags, system info, behaviour rules, workflow, session lock data.

auto_load_governance.auto_load_ceiling_note: Maximum auto-load chunks beyond session_loader. Currently using 1 (behaviour_personality). Headroom for 2 more before warning triggers. Soft ceiling — Claude warns, user decides, decision logged in versioning.
auto_load_governance.session_chunk_ceiling_note: Maximum total chunks loaded in a single session. At 16 total chunks in KB, loading 12 in one session indicates either full KB review (declare intent) or unintentional accumulation. Soft ceiling — Claude warns, user decides.
auto_load_governance.kb_review_exemption: Declaring session intent as KB review suspends the session chunk ceiling for that session. Auto-load ceiling still applies. Lock protocol still available if erratic behavior flagged.
auto_load_governance.kb_definition: KB = Knowledge Base. The structured memory system stored in this repository.

notes._summary: General working notes, memory system drafts, session logs (cross-project). Project-specific notes live under projects.<name>.*

entities._summary: Named entities not tied to a specific project: people, organizations, artifacts.

projects._summary: All project-specific content. Each project has its own subtopic namespace.

perspective._summary: Representation Space Traversal AI — a novel architecture where traversal of data representation space is the primary computational mechanism.
perspective._note: Project findings may eventually inform the MemoryAccess memory architecture itself.
